landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links present and correctly ordered (skip to content + skip to navigation), <aside> labels the sidebar, both <nav> elements are distinctly labelled ('Primary' / 'Secondary'), <main> wraps only the page-unique content, and <section> uses aria-labelledby. No landmarks are overused.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> 'Projects', followed by an <h2> 'All projects' section heading, then <h3> for each project card name — a clean, unbroken hierarchy. Although the key expectation mentions h2 for card names, the h3 level is correct given the intermediate section heading and represents stronger document structure.

element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match:

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where native HTML cannot provide semantics: aria-label on both <nav> landmarks (required), aria-current='page' on the active nav link, aria-labelledby on <section> and <article> elements, and tabindex='-1' on <main> to support the skip link. No redundant role attributes anywhere.

list_semantics.justification: Nav items are in <ul> within each <nav>, project cards are in a <ul> (count is meaningful), and card metadata uses <dl>/<dt>/<dd> — an excellent choice for the key-value status/date/members data. All list types are appropriate.

expectations_met: Sidebar navigation uses <nav> with a descriptive label ('Primary')
<main> wraps only the projects content area — sidebar is outside it
Both 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation' links are the first focusable elements
Both <nav> elements have distinct aria-labels ('Primary' and 'Secondary')
Nav items are in a <ul> list
User dropdown uses <button popovertarget> with the Popover API — no <details> or heavy ARIA
'Profile' and 'Preferences' are links; 'Sign Out' is a <button>
Project cards are in a <ul> list
Status badges carry text content ('Active', 'Paused', 'Complete') — not colour alone
All dates use <time> with datetime attribute
Secondary nav section has a label ('Secondary') distinguishing it from primary nav
Logo is an with
expectations_missed: Key expectation states project card names should be <h2>, but they are <h3> — this is caused by an intermediate <h2> 'All projects' section heading. The resulting h1→h2→h3 hierarchy is valid and arguably better structured, but does not literally match the expectation.
additional_observations: Excellent use of <dl>/<dt>/<dd> for card metadata (status, date, members) — a semantically strong choice rarely made
<article aria-labelledby> for each project card correctly marks self-contained content and provides an accessible name
Avatar img alt='' is correct because the button's visible text 'Alex Chen' already provides the accessible label
tabindex='-1' on <main> ensures the skip link can programmatically focus the landmark
